sections.paragraph:
-

(1) to be or become satisfied or satiated (Arab. شَبِعَ id. I place the primary idea in abundance of drink; compare סָבָא; although in the common use of the language this verb is more employed as to food than as to drink) Frequently used of one satisfied with food, Deu 31:20 Rth 2:14 Isa 44:16, etc.; more rarely of one who is so with drink, i.q. רָוָה Amo 4:8 hence used of a well-watered land, Pro 30:16 Psa 104:16. It is ascribed to the soul (see ופְנְ No. 2 ), Ecc 6:3 and metaph. to the eye which is satisfied with seeing, Ecc 1:8 compare Isa 53:11 Psa 17:15. Constr. absol. Hos 4:10 Psa 37:19 followed by an acc. of the thing, as שָׂבַע לֶחֶם to be satisfied with bread, Exo 16:12 Job 27:14 Ecc 5:9 followed by מִן Pro 14:14 18:20 Job 31:31 followed by בְּ Psa 65:5 88:4 followed by לְ before an inf., Ecc 1:8. Metaph. it is said, to be satisfied with money, Ecc 5:9 with shame, Lam 3:30 Hab 2:16 with contempt, Psa 123:4 with calamity, Psa 88:4.

(2) Sometimes the notion of weariness and loathing is added, Isa 1:11 Job 7:4 Pro 25:17. שָׂבַע יָמִים to be full of days, 1Ch 23:1 2Ch 24:15.

Piel, to satisfy, Eze 7:19 followed by two acc. of pers. and thing, Psa 90:14.

Hiphil, to satisfy; followed by an acc. of pers. Psa 107:9 followed by two acc. of pers. and thing, Psa 132:15 followed by מִן of the thing, Eze 32:4 Ps. 81:17 followed by בְּ of thing, Psa 103:5. Once followed by לְ of pers. and acc. of thing, Psa 145:16. Metaph. Psa 91:16.
